title: Audit reveals failures in Walsh administration's payroll modernization project
summary: A 36-page audit of the Walsh administration's payroll modernization project—which cost the city over $8 million in five years—reveals significant oversight failures, unusual procurement waivers, and incomplete system deployment. Key issues include reliance on external consultants (EY), non-cooperating former staff (Caliva, May), unpaid vendor claims, and continued employee reliance on paper checks despite modernization efforts.

political_signal.description: Public audit findings expose significant project management and procurement failures under Mayor Walsh administration; escalated by whistleblower exposure and council discussion; unresolved vendor payment disputes with EY signal potential legal/reputational risk.

processing_notes: Dollar amount extracted as '$8 million' not '$8'
Whistleblower reference (October 2024) indicates prior escalation trigger
Two key witnesses (Caliva, May) declined interviews; Caliva's departure coincided with EY contract termination—suggests possible internal conflict
Redactions cited for cybersecurity and EY dispute areas indicate ongoing sensitivity
Core CIO concern: IT project governance, vendor management, and system deployment failure
Council members (Gethers) expressing surprise suggests information asymmetry or communication breakdown
